techniques.evidence_quote: This particular incident involved the use of compromised IAM user access keys (AKIA) to gain initial access to a cloud account
InvokeModel requests returning specific error codes indicative of access checks
the attacker used the high-level permissions of the IAM user to create new IAM users, along with IAM access keys and a policy granting Bedrock permissions
the actor created an IAM user and attached a newly created policy
create new IAM users, along with IAM access keys
originating from a Proton VPN IP address
attempting to hijack LLM models across multiple AWS environments

attack_chain.description: Attacker used compromised IAM user access keys (AKIA) to gain initial access to the AWS account, connecting from Proton VPN IP addresses.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: This particular incident involved the use of compromised IAM user access keys (AKIA) to gain initial access to a cloud account and invoke Bedrock models

evidence_chunks.quote: This particular incident involved the use of compromised IAM user access keys (AKIA) to gain initial access to a cloud account and invoke Bedrock models
This particular incident involved the use of compromised IAM user access keys (AKIA) to gain initial access to a cloud account and invoke Bedrock models

attack_chain.description: Attacker attempted to invoke multiple Bedrock models (including various Claude variants) likely using a Python script to enumerate which models were accessible.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: This user made numerous unsuccessful attempts to invoke Bedrock models, likely using a Python script

evidence_chunks.quote: This user made numerous unsuccessful attempts to invoke Bedrock models, likely using a Python script
This user made numerous unsuccessful attempts to invoke Bedrock models, likely using a Python script

attack_chain.description: Using the compromised Administrator-level IAM user, the attacker created new IAM users following a naming regex `^[A-Z][a-z]{5}[0-9]{3}$`.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the attacker used the high-level permissions of the IAM user to create new IAM users

evidence_chunks.quote: the attacker used the high-level permissions of the IAM user to create new IAM users
the attacker used the high-level permissions of the IAM user to create new IAM users

attack_chain.description: Attacker created IAM access keys for the newly created IAM users to maintain persistent programmatic access.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: create new IAM users, along with IAM access keys

evidence_chunks.quote: create new IAM users, along with IAM access keys
create new IAM users, along with IAM access keys

attack_chain.description: Attacker created an IAM policy named `New_Policy` granting bedrock:InvokeModel, bedrock:InvokeModelWithResponseStream, and bedrock:GetModelInvocationLoggingConfiguration on all resources, and attached it to the new IAM users.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: a policy granting Bedrock permissions... The IAM policy name was `New_Policy`

attack_chain.description: Attacker created a console login profile for the new IAM user, observed via the `PutUseCaseForModelAccess` console API call, likely to complete the Bedrock LLM use-case/agreement process.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the attacker created a console profile for the new IAM user, likely to complete the LLM agreement process. This activity was evidenced by the AWS API call `PutUseCaseForModelAccess`

evidence_chunks.quote: the attacker created a console profile for the new IAM user, likely to complete the LLM agreement process. This activity was evidenced by the AWS API call `PutUseCaseForModelAccess`
the attacker created a console profile for the new IAM user, likely to complete the LLM agreement process. This activity was evidenced by the AWS API call `PutUseCaseForModelAccess`

attack_chain.description: Attacker attempted to request access to specific foundation models via `CreateFoundationModelAgreement` API calls, which were blocked by Service Control Policies (SCPs).
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the user attempted to request access to specific models using the `CreateFoundationModelAgreement` API call. However, these requests were blocked by the account's Service Control Policies (SCPs)

attack_chain.description: Attacker attempted to invoke Bedrock foundation models (Claude v2, Claude 3 Sonnet, Claude 3.5 Sonnet variants) using the newly created user credentials to hijack the LLMs (LLMJacking). Attempts were ultimately blocked by SCPs.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the attacker tried to invoke the model again, this time using the new user credentials. These attempts failed as well, due to the SCP blocking the `CreateFoundationModelAgreement` and `InvokeModel` API calls

evidence_chunks.quote: the attacker tried to invoke the model again, this time using the new user credentials. These attempts failed as well, due to the SCP blocking the `CreateFoundationModelAgreement` and `InvokeModel` API calls

the attacker tried to invoke the model again, this time using the new user credentials. These attempts failed as well, due to the SCP blocking the `CreateFoundationModelAgreement` and `InvokeModel` API calls

attack_chain.description: After initial attempts failed, the attacker created two additional IAM users following the same naming scheme and repeated the process, again unsuccessfully.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the attacker created two additional IAM users following the same naming scheme and repeated the process with them

evidence_chunks.quote: the attacker created two additional IAM users following the same naming scheme and repeated the process with them
the attacker created two additional IAM users following the same naming scheme and repeated the process with them

initial_access_vector: Compromised IAM user access keys (AKIA) used from Proton VPN IP addresses to access AWS account
persistence_mechanism: Creation of new IAM users (matching regex ^[A-Z][a-z]{5}[0-9]{3}$) with newly generated access keys, console profiles, and attached `New_Policy` granting Bedrock permissions
privilege_escalation: Abuse of a compromised IAM user with Administrator Access permissions to create additional IAM users, access keys, and policies granting Bedrock model invocation rights
